fix_hint: Add parameter and return annotations to public/internal function signatures.

title: Local require outside import section
standard_violated: sk-code OpenCode code organization requires dependencies in the import section, ordered built-in, third-party, local.
fix_hint: Move the fanout-salvage require into section 1 with the other local requires.

title: Unnumbered TypeScript section divider
standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript and universal checklists require numbered ALL-CAPS section dividers.
fix_hint: Use numbered dividers such as 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS, 2. CONSTANTS.

title: Exported class lacks TSDoc
standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist requires TSDoc on exported classes.
fix_hint: Add a short TSDoc summary before the exported error class.

title: Exported interface lacks TSDoc
standard_violated: sk-code TypeScript checklist requires TSDoc on exported interfaces.
fix_hint: Add TSDoc summaries for exported public data contracts.

title: CommonJS used in ESM plugin
standard_violated: sk-code JavaScript quality standards §2 and JavaScript checklist P1: .opencode/plugins/* must follow the OpenCode plugin ESM loader path with export default.
evidence: .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:17 uses require(), .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:20 uses __dirname, while .opencode/plugins/session-cleanup.js:23 exports default.
fix_hint: Use ESM imports and derive the directory from import.meta.url/fileURLToPath.

title: Script-level state uses lowercase globals
standard_violated: Shell Style Guide Naming: constants and script-level variables use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.
fix_hint: Rename script-level arrays/state to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE or localize them.

title: CJS script lacks boxed module header
standard_violated: sk-code OpenCode JavaScript Style Guide: JavaScript files must use the boxed module header and .js/.cjs strict mode immediately after the header
evidence: .opencode/commands/doctor/scripts/parent-skill-check.cjs:1-26 starts with shebang/JSDoc and puts 'use strict' at line 26; no boxed module header
fix_hint: Use the standard JS boxed header after the shebang, then place 'use strict' before implementation code.
